title: Ned N Ednas Blend Agenda

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: The iconic D'oh sound
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, iconic but not structurally complex

jokes.text: the Nathan Lane of the New Testament, Jesus Christ
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jesus to Broadway star Nathan Lane
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronistic comparison mixing religion and Broadway, original but somewhat forced

jokes.text: not the I Once Had Three Shows on Broadway Simultaneously But I Blew All My Money on Coke and Now Here I Am Play
jokes.setup: Director contrasting with the Passion Play
jokes.punchline: Overly specific, dark backstory about theatrical failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from generic to hyperspecific failure, dark but earned humor

jokes.text: Hi. My name is Milhouse Van Houten, and I am auditioning for the role of Child Who Points.
jokes.punchline: Auditioning for the incredibly minor role of 'Child Who Points'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character moment - earnest overpreparation for tiny role

jokes.text: I prepared right!
jokes.setup: Director asks Milhouse to point stage left
jokes.punchline: Milhouse admits he prepared to point in the wrong direction
jokes.explanation: Classic Milhouse incompetence - over-prepared for the wrong thing

jokes.text: Man Who Buys Ticket, But Doesn't Show Up
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his role in the play
jokes.punchline: His 'role' is someone who doesn't even attend
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Homer's character and commitment level, perfectly Homer-esque laziness

jokes.text: Krusty vs. the F.D.A. Game Six of the 2011 World Series. Hitler mashups! And Lenny's story about Cancun.
jokes.setup: Bart listing better stories than the Passion
jokes.punchline: Random escalation from pop culture to Hitler mashups to Lenny's vacation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but relies on randomness, typical Bart irreverence

jokes.text: That thing's hilarious. Not so hilarious for me. I nearly lost a toe.
jokes.setup: Carl praising Lenny's Cancun story as hilarious
jokes.punchline: Lenny's deadpan revelation that he nearly lost a toe
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure with Lenny's characteristic understated delivery

jokes.text: That's what you get for having a monkey give you a foot massage. My bucket list is my business.
jokes.setup: Carl's explanation for Lenny's toe injury
jokes.punchline: Absurd monkey massage scenario, Lenny's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual scenario with good character follow-up, memorable and quotable

jokes.text: Credits include Jesus in the Passion Play last year, Jesus in the Passion Play the year before that, Jesus, Jesus, a kindly hobo who turns out to be-- you guessed it-- Saint Peter
jokes.punchline: He's only ever played Jesus or religious figures, with escalating repetition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character comedy - his religious typecasting revealed through escalation

jokes.text: I'm so bored I'm ready to introduce myself to my two assistants here.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he doesn't even know his own assistants
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan delivery about workplace alienation

jokes.text: But-but-but... Jesus didn't stammer! His voice hit the back of the theater!
jokes.setup: Ned being given a non-speaking role
jokes.punchline: He protests by stammering while claiming Jesus didn't stammer
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic structure - Ned undermines his own point while making it

jokes.text: Just like me on Twitter!
jokes.punchline: Anachronistic Twitter reference from ultra-traditional Ned
jokes.explanation: Mild anachronistic humor playing against Ned's character

jokes.text: And he was tragically killed while still in his 30s... D'oh! ...after an all-night dinner. Woo-hoo! I'm your Jesus! Me!
jokes.punchline: Homer realizes he fits the description and gets excited
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection as Homer realizes he matches Jesus's profile, perfect character moment

jokes.text: You understand why God would have forsaken him.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting God would abandon Jesus if he were like Homer
jokes.explanation: Dark theological humor with perfect economy and character insight

jokes.text: I guess this is just my cross to bear.
jokes.punchline: Religious pun about bearing crosses in context of Passion play
jokes.explanation: Predictable but well-executed religious pun, perfect for Ned's character

jokes.text: Whee! Whee!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he'll take Jesus role seriously
jokes.punchline: Immediately acting childish while saying 'Very seriously. Whee!'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction - saying he's serious while being completely frivolous

jokes.text: It's like wearing a Florida State jersey to a Florida game.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining how messing with Jesus angers people
jokes.punchline: College football rivalry analogy
jokes.explanation: Good analogy from Lisa, showing her understanding of both religion and sports rivalries

jokes.text: Does Jesus have a big part in this thing? He's in every scene. What? Why do I succeed at everything I audition for?
jokes.setup: Homer asking about Jesus's role in the Passion
jokes.punchline: Homer's obliviousness and false modesty about his 'success'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - doesn't know Jesus is the main character of his own story

jokes.text: You can probably plead this down to crucifixion.
jokes.setup: Pilate as modern lawyer discussing Jesus's case
jokes.punchline: Treating crucifixion as a lesser sentence to plead down to
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor mixing legal jargon with crucifixion, excellent economy

jokes.text: Thou sayest it. He's good.
jokes.punchline: Pilate's modern approval of Homer's delivery
jokes.explanation: Good anachronistic humor with Pilate as modern critic

jokes.text: Wow, I'm so moved I renounce science, with the facts, and the peer reviews, and the hypotheses tested by evidence.
jokes.setup: Frink being moved by Homer's performance
jokes.punchline: The scientist listing all the things he's supposedly giving up
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony with Frink's scientific specificity

jokes.text: I'll take you with me, old man!
jokes.punchline: Homer/Jesus threatening violence
jokes.explanation: Homer's aggressive nature breaking through his Jesus performance

jokes.text: They cannot judge me. Only my father can judge me. I'll take you with me, old man!
jokes.punchline: Immediately turns threatening and personal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from biblical gravitas to Homer's personal threats

jokes.text: Looks like someone didn't follow the underwear rule!
jokes.setup: Visual gag implied from audience reaction
jokes.punchline: Someone can see Homer's underwear (or lack thereof) during crucifixion
jokes.explanation: Typical visual comedy setup for The Simpsons, basic but functional

jokes.text: All right! It pays to network at these things.
jokes.setup: Jesus promises thief will be in heaven
jokes.punchline: Modern networking perspective on religious salvation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern business perspective applied to biblical salvation scene

jokes.text: Out of my way, stupid Flanders! Ow!
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately reverts to insulting Ned and gets hurt
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - immediately undoing any spiritual growth

jokes.text: ♪ Who's to say This didn't happen Who's to say ♪ And curtain!
jokes.punchline: Ned tries to salvage it with improvised song
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ned's theatrical optimism in disaster

jokes.text: You remember when you said if Flanders got remarried you'd eat your hat? Uh-oh.
jokes.punchline: Lisa reminds him of his promise to eat his hat
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for hat-eating visual gag to follow

jokes.text: Ooh, licorice! Mmm, historically inaccurate.
jokes.setup: Homer eating his hat as promised
jokes.punchline: He critiques the historical accuracy while eating
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - focuses on irrelevant detail while fulfilling promise

jokes.text: No, but I'm sure Newt Gingrich wouldn't steer us wrong.
jokes.punchline: Ned's innocent trust in controversial political figure
jokes.explanation: Good political satire playing off Ned's naivety and Gingrich's reputation

jokes.text: Another good man bites the dust. Homer! I'm talking about this guy! I mean, I envy Flanders, once again enjoying the joys of marriage.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks it's about him, has to clarify multiple times
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on Homer's self-centeredness

jokes.text: 'Cause everyone in this town makes such a big heckabaloo out of everything. Nedding bells are ringing. See what happens when Teachy met Preachy
jokes.setup: Edna complaining about town making big deal of things
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by media circus with puns
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure with immediate contradiction and typical Springfield media puns

jokes.text: I tied them together with used dental floss, as per your memo!
jokes.setup: Edna asking about wig glue on shoes
jokes.punchline: Seymour's gross and overly specific alternative
jokes.explanation: Classic Seymour pathetic behavior, gross but character-appropriate

jokes.text: And thank you for 'cigarette mommy.' Oh, honey, please don't call me that. Okay, 'do-over mommy.'
jokes.setup: Prayer thanking for new mother figure
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent but loaded descriptions of Edna
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from child's perspective, good character work with loaded terminology

jokes.text: Which one? 'Homer' or 'fat ass'? They're both bad, and I suspect you know it!
jokes.punchline: Bart's false confusion creates even worse situation
jokes.explanation: Good Bart mischief moment with smart structure making things worse

jokes.text: Only God can make words. Right, right. Yahtzee? Dice are Satan's bones.
jokes.setup: Edna suggesting games to Flanders boys
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd religious objections to normal activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of religious extremism, 'Dice are Satan's bones' is brilliantly absurd and quotable

jokes.text: Thank God, a real kid. I don't. I'm just bored watching worms drown.
jokes.setup: Edna expressing relief at Bart's arrival
jokes.punchline: Bart's casually dark response about his entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good dark Bart humor contrasting with Flanders boys' sheltered nature

jokes.text: Skipping is cool! Especially when you're holding hands with your brother.
jokes.setup: Bart giving advice about acting normal
jokes.punchline: Boys' innocent response shows how sheltered they are
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing the boys' innocent worldview

jokes.text: Snippy? How can you say that in those shoes?
jokes.punchline: Helen immediately proves the point while denying it
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure with Helen proving Marge's point immediately

jokes.text: The left-gifted bidextrous and transhanded community. 'Ourcissors, Ourselves.' Death to Righties. We have to live among them. To live among them is to die.
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurdity of left-handed activism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay misunderstanding with escalating militant left-handed activism

jokes.text: Look, I'm a chalk jockey, too, so don't slap ketchup on bread and tell me it's Pizza Friday.
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor about educational quality
jokes.explanation: Excellent teacher-to-teacher insult with memorable, specific imagery

jokes.text: Now I can bring a latte and leave the cup on the desk. Ha!
jokes.setup: Edna excited to be the parent at parent-teacher conference
jokes.punchline: Her idea of parental authority is leaving coffee cups
jokes.explanation: Edna's specific and petty vision of parental privilege from teacher perspective

jokes.text: Well, I'll be a monkey's unrelated creature.
jokes.setup: Learning Edna teaches at public school
jokes.punchline: Religious school's evolution-denying version of common expression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay showing religious school's anti-evolution stance through modified idiom

jokes.text: Well, first of all, I think talking dogs are the spawn of the devil.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why they're at secular college
jokes.punchline: Bizarre religious objection to cartoon talking animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned absurdity - finding religious objections to harmless things

jokes.text: Comparative religious studies.
jokes.setup: Ned's relief that Todd studied religious studies
jokes.punchline: The 'comparative' part makes it problematic for Ned
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - single word destroys Ned's relief

jokes.text: Why are you parking yourself? Why are you parking yourself? We haven't taken in one car all day.
jokes.punchline: Attendants with no business arguing with each other
jokes.explanation: Minor background gag about unsuccessful parking attendants

jokes.text: I'm married to the sea, and I'm seeing two of the Great Lakes on the side. I won't say which ones, but it's eerie how superior they are.
jokes.punchline: Extended nautical metaphor with Lake Superior pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor with clever Lake Superior pun, perfectly nautical

jokes.text: Mine's a sassy princess who speaks her mind. Mine's a duck with no pants.
jokes.punchline: Innocent descriptions that sound problematic out of context
jokes.explanation: Good innocent children humor with adult implications

jokes.text: Yeah, chillax, Daddy.
jokes.punchline: Complete transformation from their previous speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Good character development joke showing the boys' transformation

jokes.text: Four acts... blasphemy.
jokes.punchline: Treating four-act structure as religiously offensive
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on episode structure with Moe's typical dramatic overreaction

jokes.text: Yesterday I gave in to the one thing I vowed I would never have in my marriage: strong feeling.
jokes.punchline: He's ashamed of having emotions in marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ned pathologizing normal human emotions

jokes.text: Because his avatar is Darth Maul, the second-coolest Darth. And what he says is true because it's in all caps.
jokes.punchline: Absurd criteria for believing online strangers
jokes.explanation: Good satire of internet culture through Homer's simple logic

jokes.text: I'm keeping a dolphin in my bathtub!
jokes.setup: Family pressuring Bart to reveal his secret
jokes.punchline: Completely unexpected and absurd confession
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and escalation - completely unexpected reveal

jokes.text: They're as happy as a ribbon in a Bible.
jokes.setup: Edna describing the Simpsons' approach to parenting
jokes.punchline: Ned-style religious metaphor coming from secular Edna
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Edna adopting Ned's speech patterns

jokes.text: ♪ Hens love roosters, geese love ganders Everyone else loves Ned Flanders ♪
jokes.punchline: Innocent, wholesome rap about being universally loved
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - wholesome rap that's completely sincere and endearing

tmdb_overview: When Marge learns Ned and Edna got married, she offers to throw them a reception; Edna changes some rules to help Ned's kids become more socially acceptable.
